title: NEET UG 2026
meta_description: NEET UG 2026 — medical entrance exam for MBBS, BDS, AYUSH. NTA exam pattern, syllabus, cutoff, counselling process, and preparation.

hero.one_liner: India's single medical entrance exam — NEET UG is the only gateway to MBBS, BDS, and AYUSH admissions in all government and private medical colleges.

what_is_it: NEET UG (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test — Undergraduate) is India's single entrance exam for admission to MBBS, BDS, BAMS (Ayurveda), BHMS (Homeopathy), BUMS (Unani), and related medical/dental/AYUSH courses in all government and private medical colleges across India (excluding only AIIMS and JIPMER, which now accept NEET scores too). With 24+ lakh applicants competing for approximately 1,10,000 MBBS seats, NEET is India's largest entrance exam by sheer number of candidates — more than JEE Main. It is conducted once per year in pen-and-paper mode (OMR sheet), not online.

NEET score is the determining factor for admission to: Government medical colleges through state quota and All-India Quota (AIQ), Private medical colleges, Deemed universities with medical programs, AIIMS institutions (post-acceptance of NEET), JIPMER (post-acceptance of NEET), and medical colleges abroad recognized by Indian Medical Council. Without NEET qualification, no medical admission is possible in India — NEET is completely monopolistic. The exam tests Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (Botany + Zoology) with 200 MCQs in 3 hours 20 minutes, totaling 720 marks.

NEET 2026 exam is scheduled for May 3, 2026, from 2 to 5 PM. Application registration began February 8, 2026, with the last submission date being March 11, 2026. The exam is conducted in pen-and-paper format using OMR sheets — candidates mark answers in bubble sheets with pencils, unlike modern online exams. This is intentional — OMR format ensures no technical glitches and is equitable for candidates from diverse educational backgrounds. The result is declared typically 2-3 weeks post-exam, followed by state and AIQ counselling in July-August.

Biology dominates NEET scoring — 360 out of 720 marks. Most successful NEET candidates score 320-360 in Biology alone. Physics is the toughest for most aspirants, followed by Chemistry. The qualitative difference: Biology requires depth of knowledge and memory (NCERT-based), Physics requires conceptual clarity (application-based), Chemistry requires balanced memory + concepts. Many students over-prepare Physics while under-preparing Biology — a strategic error. The winner in NEET is who masters Biology first.

key_details.label: Education Requirement
key_details.value: 12th pass with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology. Min 50% aggregate (40% for SC/ST/OBC).

key_details.label: Age Limit
key_details.value: Minimum 17 years as of December 31, 2026. No upper age limit (Supreme Court 2023 ruling).

key_details.label: Exam Attempts
key_details.value: No official limit on number of attempts (as per current rules)

key_details.label: Exam Mode
key_details.value: Offline — Pen and Paper (OMR sheet) — NOT computer-based

exam_pattern.tier_name: NEET UG Paper (3 hours 20 minutes — 200 minutes)
exam_pattern.description: 200 MCQs across Physics, Chemistry, Botany, and Zoology. Each subject has Section A (35 questions, compulsory) + Section B (15 questions, attempt any 10). Total questions: 180 (35×4 = 140 compulsory + 40 optional). OMR-based offline exam.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: +4 for each correct answer. -1 for each wrong answer. No marking for unattempted questions.

major_posts.department: AIIMS Delhi, Maulana Azad, Grant Medical, Top Government Colleges

major_posts.pay_level: Same post-degree salary as government MBBS
major_posts.in_hand_salary: Fees: ₹5-25 lakh/year for basic colleges. Placements: ₹40,000-80,000 starting
major_posts.department: Top private colleges: Manipal, CMC Vellore, Kasturba Medical, etc.

extra_sections.heading: Post-NEET — Counselling and Admission Process
content:
AIQ vs State Quota

NEET score is used for two separate allocation processes: (1) All-India Quota (AIQ) — 15% of seats in government colleges are allocated nationally. Managed by MCC (Medical Counselling Committee).
Registration by all-India rank. (2) State Quota — 85% of seats are state-specific. Managed by state authorities.
Registration by state rank. You appear in both.
AIQ counselling happens July-August. State counselling happens August-September.
Multiple rounds of seat allocation happen — if you don't get your first choice, you can participate in subsequent rounds.

Counselling Rounds

Counselling is conducted in multiple rounds (typically 3-6 rounds depending on state). Each round: (1) Login and fill choices (colleges/courses in preference order), (2) Wait for provisional merit list, (3) Verify documents if your name appears, (4) Report to college for admission.
If you don't get admission in Round 1, you can participate in Round 2, 3, etc. Floating seats (unfilled seats from previous rounds) come into play in later rounds.
Many candidates get their choice college in Round 2 or 3.

Deemed Universities & Private Colleges

Deemed universities accept NEET scores but have their own counselling. Apply directly to their websites.

Fees vary massively: JIPMER (deemed, top quality) ₹3-5 lakh/year, Manipal/CMC Vellore ₹10-20 lakh/year, private colleges ₹5-25 lakh/year. Quality is NOT directly proportional to fees — research college reputation, placement data, and medical council recognition before committing to high fees.

extra_sections.heading: NEET Vs JEE Main — Comparison for Aspirants

preparation_tips: NCERT is the BIBLE for NEET — 90-95% of questions come directly from NCERT 11th and 12th textbooks. Biology questions often come word-for-word from NCERT diagrams and definitions. Read NCERT Biology line-by-line multiple times. Successful NEET toppers read NCERT Bio 7-10 times before exam. Memorize diagrams, definitions, and classification systems verbatim.

Biology is your scoring subject — it's 50% of total marks (360/720). Most NEET toppers score 340-360 in Biology. Physics is toughest for aspirants (average: 120-150). Chemistry is middle (average: 140-170). Strategic approach: Dedicate 40% of prep time to Biology, 30% to Chemistry, 30% to Physics. This gives you Biology: 340+, Chemistry: 150+, Physics: 130+ = 620+ (99+ percentile).

Physics requires conceptual clarity from HC Verma or Physics Wallah videos, followed by MCQ practice from question banks. Don't memorize physics — understand principles. Focus on: Mechanics (25%), Optics (15%), Electrostatics+Current (20%), Modern Physics (10%), Thermodynamics (10%). Ignore advanced topics like superconductivity. Physics is only 25% of NEET — master the high-frequency chapters.

Chemistry has three components: Inorganic (reactions, periodic table), Organic (mechanisms, named reactions), Physical (thermodynamics, kinetics). Inorganic requires fact memorization — use charts, tables, periodic table notes. Organic requires understanding reaction mechanisms — MS Chouhan book is standard. Physical requires formula-based problem solving. Allocate prep: 30% Inorganic, 35% Organic, 35% Physical.

Practice with OMR sheets — NEET is offline pen-and-paper. Bubble filling speed and accuracy matter. Filling wrong bubbles or skipping bubbles causes mark loss. Practice with printed OMR sheets weekly. Time pressure is real — 3h 20m for 200 questions = 1 minute per question average. Time management: spend 1-1.5 minutes on easy questions, skip hard ones initially, revisit if time permits.

faqs.question: Is NEET the only way to become a doctor in India?
faqs.answer: Yes, NEET is mandatory for MBBS/BDS admission in all government and private medical colleges in India. Even AIIMS and JIPMER, which historically had their own exams, now accept NEET scores. The only alternative: Study MBBS abroad (China, Philippines, Russia) and clear FMGE (Foreign Medical Graduates Exam) to practice in India. But this is costly (₹20-40 lakh) and has lower success rate on FMGE than clearing NEET and studying in India.

faqs.question: How many times can I attempt NEET?
faqs.answer: Currently, no official limit on attempts. You can appear every year as long as you meet age eligibility (minimum 17 years as of Dec 31). Many successful NEET candidates clear it on 2nd or 3rd attempt. Dropping a year for NEET preparation is socially acceptable in India — many aspirants take deliberate breaks to focus. However, realize that scores don't carry over — each year is a fresh exam.

faqs.question: NEET vs JEE — which is harder?
faqs.answer: Different skill types. JEE tests conceptual application and problem-solving at advanced levels. NEET tests memory, depth of knowledge, and standard-level problem solving. JEE seats per applicant: 0.5%. NEET seats per applicant: 4.4%. Statistically, JEE is harder to crack, but subjectively, NEET has tougher competition numbers. A student weak in logical reasoning should choose NEET (Biology-based); a student weak in memorization should choose JEE (concept-based).

faqs.question: Can I get MBBS with 500 marks out of 720?
faqs.answer: 500/720 (69.4%) gets you a government MBBS seat in many states through state quota counselling, especially in less competitive states (NE states, J&K, Tier-2 state capitals). For competitive states (Delhi, UP, Rajasthan), you need 550+ for government MBBS. For private colleges, 500 is sufficient for admissions. For BDS (dental), 500 is excellent — you'll get government BDS in most states. Score 500 is 97 percentile approximately — sufficient for medical education, not for top colleges.

faqs.question: What's the cost of MBBS in India?
faqs.answer: Government MBBS: ₹10,000-1,00,000/year (total 5.5 years = ₹50,000-5,00,000). Private MBBS: ₹5-25 lakh/year (total 5.5 years = ₹27-137 lakh). However, education loans cover 100% of fees at low interest (6-7%). Post-MBBS salary starts ₹60,000/month as junior doctor. After MD/MS (specialization), income reaches ₹1,00,000+/month. ROI calculation: Loan repaid in 2-3 years. MBBS is a high-ROI investment.

faqs.question: I scored 90 percentile in NEET. Can I get government MBBS?
faqs.answer: 90 percentile is approximately 545+ marks — yes, you qualify for government MBBS in most states through state quota counselling. However, actual college allocation depends on your state rank and state-specific cutoffs. In competitive states (Delhi, Tamil Nadu), 90 percentile may not secure top government colleges but will secure mid-tier colleges. In less competitive states, 90 percentile secures good government MBBS. Always research your state's previous year cutoffs.

syllabus.subject: Physics
syllabus.topics: Mechanics, thermodynamics, optics, electrostatics, current electricity, magnetism, electromagnetic waves, dual nature of matter, atoms & nuclei, semiconductor electronics. NEET physics is NCERT-focused — 90% questions from NCERT examples and exercises.

syllabus.subject: Chemistry
syllabus.topics: Physical chemistry (solutions, electrochemistry, kinetics, surface chemistry), organic chemistry (hydrocarbons, haloalkanes, alcohols, amines, biomolecules, polymers), inorganic chemistry (p-block, d-block, coordination compounds, metallurgy). NCERT is the bible for NEET chemistry.

syllabus.subject: Biology (Botany + Zoology)
syllabus.topics: Diversity of living organisms, structural organization, cell biology, plant physiology, human physiology, reproduction, genetics & evolution, biology & human welfare, biotechnology, ecology & environment. Biology carries 360/720 marks — master this section.

books.title: NCERT Biology (11th + 12th)
books.author: NCERT
books.subject: Biology (most important — read line by line)

books.title: MTG Objective NCERT at Your Fingertips
books.author: MTG Editorial Board
books.subject: All subjects (MCQ practice)

study_plan.tasks: Read NCERT Biology line-by-line — every diagram, every example, every box item. NCERT Chemistry — focus on reactions, named reactions, exceptions. Physics — solve all NCERT examples and exercises. Make handwritten notes from NCERT.

Trueman's Biology for additional MCQs and diagrams. H.C. Verma for physics problem-solving. Solve MTG Fingertips for all 3 subjects. Focus on Biology (50% weightage) — genetics, ecology, and human physiology are highest-yield chapters.

Solve previous 10 years NEET papers. Weekly full mock tests. Revise NCERT diagrams and important tables. Focus on Biology assertion-reason and matching questions. Last month: daily mock + NCERT revision only.
